own_review.issue: long-to-int truncation: `int len = (int)(count() - transferred())` and `int offset = (int)(position() + transferred())`, plus the `transferred` field itself is `int`. For FileRegions/offsets >= 2GB the splice offset is truncated, silently sending wrong file data or failing with EINVAL (negative len). DefaultFileRegion supports long counts; epoll/NIO paths handle >2GB.

When fileRegion.open() throws IOException, handleWriteError(e) is called and 0 is returned. Caller AbstractIoUringChannel.scheduleWrite() has `assert numOutstandingWrites > 0` (line ~332) which fires AssertionError with -ea (netty CI runs with assertions); without assertions the write path relies entirely on handleWriteError closing the channel.

-ECANCELED is not special-cased for the FileRegion path. The ByteBuf path returns true for Native.ERRNO_ECANCELED_NEGATIVE, but for IoUringFileRegion `ioResult("io_uring splice", res)` throws NativeIoException for ECANCELED, which is routed to handleWriteError -> spurious exception/close when an outstanding splice is cancelled (e.g. cancelOutstandingWrites during deregistration/close).

The new guard `msgCount > 1 && in.current() instanceof ByteBuf` also governs IoUringDatagramChannel, whose messages are DatagramPacket/AddressedEnvelope (not ByteBuf). Multi-message datagram flushes that previously went through batched scheduleWriteMultiple (writeProcessor) now degrade to one-write-at-a-time scheduleWriteSingle — a performance regression unrelated to FileRegion.

JNI_OnLoad now hard-fails (JNI_ERR -> entire io_uring native library unusable) if DefaultFileRegion.file, sun.nio.ch.FileChannelImpl.fd or java.io.FileDescriptor.fd/descriptor cannot be resolved, coupling the whole transport to JDK internals. Additionally getFd0 applies FileChannelImpl's fieldID via GetObjectField to whatever FileChannel the user put in DefaultFileRegion — a custom FileChannel subclass yields undefined behavior (no IsInstanceOf check).

`result == 0 && fileRegion.count() > 0` uses total count() rather than remaining bytes, and EAGAIN (Errors.ioResult returns 0) is funneled into the same EOF/validateFileRegion branch; works incidentally (validate passes, POLLOUT rescheduled) but conflates two distinct conditions.

`transferred += result` counts bytes spliced into the intermediate pipe as transferred before they reach the socket; transferred()/progress can over-report on mid-transfer failure, and progress() is only invoked for socket-stage completions.

Missing `assumeTrue(IoUring.isAvailable())` @BeforeAll guard (unlike IoUringFileTest/IoUringSocketFileRegionTest) — test errors instead of skipping where io_uring is unavailable; group.shutdownGracefully() is not awaited.

Leftover debug artifact: commented-out `//@Disabled("Fix me")` above a pointless override of testFileRegionCountLargerThenFile that only calls super; the `Disabled` import is now unused (checkstyle violation); copyright header says 2014 on a new 2024 file.

Public constructor of public class IoUringIoOps changed signature (spliceFdIn inserted before data) — binary/source breaking API change; also newSplice javadoc/param names use snake_case (fd_in, off_in) contrary to conventions.

Only DefaultFileRegion is wrapped; custom FileRegion implementations and kernels < 5.7 (no IORING_OP_SPLICE) fall through to super.filterOutboundMessage -> UnsupportedOperationException at write time; no transferTo() fallback. Documented limitation but worth surfacing more clearly.

points.point: IoUring.isIOUringSpliceSupported() does NOT exist in IoUring.java; the PR must add it
points.why: Fabricated absence: the full PR adds exactly this method (IoUring.java lines ~102-105) plus the IORING_SPLICE_SUPPORTED static init probing via Native.isIOUringSupportSplice — all visible in the diff.

points.point: Native.java: IORING_OP_SPLICE not in required-ops probe; clarify required vs optional
points.why: The PR already answers this: it adds isIOUringSupportSplice(ringFd) as an optional probe gating filterOutboundMessage. Moot question, and it contradicts v4's own point 1 by referencing isIOUringSpliceSupported() as the gate.

points.point: No newSplice() factory method exists in IoUringIoOps; the PR must add one
points.why: Fabricated absence: IoUringIoOps.newSplice(...) is added by the PR (with the fd_in-via-spliceFdIn field, off_in in the addr union) — exactly what v4 speculates 'needs verification'.

points.point: filterOutboundMessage accepts only DefaultFileRegion; custom FileRegion wrappers won't work
points.why: Verified: non-DefaultFileRegion falls through to super.filterOutboundMessage which throws UnsupportedOperationException; the testsuite FileRegionWrapper case is disabled via supportsCustomFileRegion()=false.

points.point: scheduleWriteSingle returns 0 after open() IOException, violating `assert numOutstandingWrites > 0` in scheduleWrite
points.why: Verified real introduced bug: AbstractIoUringChannel.scheduleWrite asserts numOutstandingWrites > 0; returning 0 fires AssertionError under -ea. Matches my own finding.

points.point: writeComplete0 FileRegion handler truncated; verify it implements progress/completion/close/retry
points.why: A hedge checklist, not a finding; the full diff contains the handler and it does not match several checklist items (no fileRegion.close() call needed — release handles it).

points.point: Guard change msgCount>1 && instanceof ByteBuf is CORRECT, matches epoll
points.why: Endorsement, no issue raised; it also overlooks that this guard regresses datagram multi-message batching (my finding).

points.point: Adding IORING_OP_SPLICE case to write completion switch is correct
points.why: Endorsement of visible code, no issue.

points.point: Verify writeOpCode set correctly for SPLICE in cancelOutstandingWrites path
points.why: Self-answered in the same sentence ('which is correct since writeOpCode = opCode is already there'); no actionable finding.

points.point: IoUringFileRegion.java not fully reviewable; list of 5 open questions
points.why: Honest hedge but contributes no verified finding; the file is fully present in the real PR.

points.point: On older kernels FileRegion write throws UnsupportedOperationException at runtime; consider warning/clearer error
points.why: Verified: with isIOUringSpliceSupported()==false, DefaultFileRegion falls through to the generic 'unsupported message type' exception at write time. Legitimate minor UX point.

points.point: supportsCustomFileRegion() guard is good; IoUringSocketFileRegionTest should override it to return false
points.why: The PR's IoUringSocketFileRegionTest already overrides it to return false — recommendation of something already done (hedged absence).

points.point: scheduleWriteSingle should add assertion/throw for unexpected message types
points.why: Pure style; filterOutboundMessage already constrains types reaching this path.

missed: Integer truncation of splice offset/len and int `transferred` field — data corruption/failure for FileRegions >= 2GB (IoUringFileRegion.spliceToPipe)
-ECANCELED not special-cased in the FileRegion writeComplete0 path (ByteBuf path returns true; splice path throws via ioResult -> handleWriteError)
scheduleWrite guard `&& instanceof ByteBuf` silently disables batched scheduleWriteMultiple for IoUringDatagramChannel (perf regression) — v4 even endorsed the guard as fully correct

JNI_OnLoad now hard-fails the entire io_uring native library if DefaultFileRegion/sun.nio.ch.FileChannelImpl/FileDescriptor field lookups fail; getFd0 applies FileChannelImpl fieldID to arbitrary FileChannel (JNI UB)
Leftover `//@Disabled("Fix me")` + pointless test override + unused Disabled import in IoUringSocketFileRegionTest

points.point: scheduleWriteSingle returns 0 on open() failure but scheduleWrite asserts numOutstandingWrites > 0 -> AssertionError
points.why: Verified real introduced bug with the correct line (~338) for the assert; matches my finding and v4's.

points.point: ECANCELED not handled gracefully for IoUringFileRegion: ioResult throws, caught and routed to handleWriteError, firing exceptionCaught for a normal cancellation; ByteBuf path returns true
points.why: Verified: Errors.ioResult throws NativeIoException for -ECANCELED; the ByteBuf branch checks Native.ERRNO_ECANCELED_NEGATIVE, the FileRegion branch does not. Accurate behavioral regression.

points.point: Pipe FD leak on error paths: closePipeIfNeeded only called in release()
points.why: Hedged ('may leak') and in practice not a leak: on any failure the ChannelOutboundBuffer fails/removes flushed messages and releases them (failFlushed -> safeRelease -> release() -> closePipeIfNeeded). Routing cleanup through release() is the intended refcount design.

points.point: Unused constants SQE_FIXED_BUFFER=40 and SQE_PERSONALITY=42 in SubmissionQueue
points.why: Factually true (only SQE_SPLICE_FD_IN is used), but they replace the equally-unused SQE_PAD_FIELD and document the SQE layout; pure style nit.

points.point: Integer truncation for files/offsets > 2GB in (int)(count()-transferred()) and (int)(position()+transferred()); remove casts since newSplice takes long
points.why: Verified: the highest-severity bug in the PR — truncated splice offset sends wrong data for >=2GB regions. Suggested fix slightly imprecise (newSplice's nbytes is int and `transferred` field is int, so more than removing casts is needed), but the core finding is correct.

points.point: validateFileRegion not called when spliceToPipe returns 0; truncation check skipped for the pipe path
points.why: False: the `result == 0 && fileRegion.count() > 0 -> validateFileRegion` branch in writeComplete0 executes before handleResult and does not inspect `data`, so it applies equally to splice-to-pipe completions. The check is NOT restricted to the splice-to-socket path.

points.point: filterOutboundMessage only handles DefaultFileRegion; custom FileRegions throw UnsupportedOperationException; acknowledged via supportsCustomFileRegion()=false; a comment would help
points.why: Limitation verified and correctly tied to the test override; minor inaccuracy that a comment 'would help' — the PR already has one ('Since we cannot use synchronous sendfile...') at that exact spot.

points.point: handleResult relies on shared `data` short for phase tracking; could conflict; use explicit state field
points.why: Speculative; the data short is the op-private payload netty itself sets in newSplice — no concrete conflict exists.

points.point: Test coverage gaps: partial splice, cancellation, mixed writes, remote close
points.why: Generic test-suggestion list; reasonable but unverified and unspecific.

missed: scheduleWrite guard `&& instanceof ByteBuf` disables batched scheduleWriteMultiple for IoUringDatagramChannel (perf regression)
JNI_OnLoad hard-fails the entire io_uring native library on field-lookup failure; getFd0 unchecked GetObjectField on arbitrary FileChannel subclass
Leftover `//@Disabled("Fix me")` + pointless override + unused Disabled import in IoUringSocketFileRegionTest
exceeds: Unused SQE_FIXED_BUFFER/SQE_PERSONALITY constants (verified accurate, though graded trivial)

qualitative: v4, working from the truncated diff, fabricated two confident absence claims ('isIOUringSpliceSupported() does NOT exist; the PR must add it' and 'No newSplice() factory exists; the PR must add one') for code the PR plainly contains, and padded the rest with hedge checklists ('not reviewable, verify that...') and endorsements; its one excellent catch (open()-failure returning 0 vs the numOutstandingWrites assert) is shared with v5. v5, with the full diff, made no must-add fabrications of v4's class — it correctly treats newSplice and the splice-support probe as existing and even cites newSplice's signature — but it has one false-absence claim of a milder kind: asserting validateFileRegion is skipped for the splice-to-pipe path when the result==0 branch is phase-agnostic (plus a moot 'add a comment' where one exists). v5 found the two most serious real bugs (2GB int truncation of the splice offset, and the ECANCELED regression) that v4 missed entirely. Both missed the datagram-channel batching regression from the scheduleWrite guard and the JNI_OnLoad hard-dependency on JDK internals. Net, v5 is clearly the stronger and more truthful review.
special_question: v5 does NOT repeat v4's fabrications: it references IoUringIoOps.newSplice() as existing (quoting its long off_in parameter) and treats the splice capability gate as present, rather than hedging around them. Its single absence-style error is different and smaller in kind: claiming the validateFileRegion truncation check is skipped for splice-to-pipe completions, when the result==0 branch in writeComplete0 applies to both phases; secondarily, it suggests adding an explanatory comment that the diff already contains.
